perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means 50% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means a percentage of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory, as determined by WPD's internal financial policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Development Fee' provided a clear calculation: 50% of Net Sales. The modified definition removes the fixed percentage and introduces ambiguity by stating it will be determined by 'WPD's internal financial policies.' This creates uncertainty and a potential contradiction. Later sections of the agreement assume a clear definition for calculating payments, leading to a contradiction. For instance, section 2.1 refers to payment of the 'Development Fee', without specifying it's determination based on WPD's internal policies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory. In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Development Fee to CNS, CNS hereby agrees to pay WPD the following payments: (i) within thirty Business Days from the date of this Agreement, CNS will make an upfront payment of $225,000 to WPD; and (ii) within thirty days of the verified achievement of the Phase II Milestone, (such verification shall be conducted by an independent third party mutually acceptable to the parties hereto), CNS will make a payment of $775,000 to WPD.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Development Fee' as a fixed percentage (50% of Net Sales), providing a clear basis for calculating payments. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the fee will be determined by WPD's internal financial policies. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a fixed 'Development Fee' when stating 'In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Development Fee to CNS...' without specifying that the calculation depends on WPD's internal policies. The contradicted section operates under the assumption of a clear and predefined Development Fee, whereas the new change makes its determination uncertain and dependent on an external factor. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty regarding payment obligations, making the contract potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.7 "Development Territory" means (i) until the Approval Achievement Date, the Sublicensed Territory, as defined in the Sublicense Agreement; and (ii) after the Approval Achievement Date, the Sublicensed Territory, as defined in the Sublicense Agreement, other than Poland.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 "Development Territory" means the geographic areas mutually agreed upon by both parties in writing, which areas may be adjusted at any time with written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Development Territory' was clearly linked to the Sublicense Agreement, specifying territories before and after the Approval Achievement Date, with specific exclusion of Poland after. The modified definition introduces ambiguity and flexibility by stating that the territory is subject to mutual agreement, which can be adjusted, creating a contradiction because the entire agreement is built on the previous clear defined territory. This contradicts sections where specific rights and obligations are tied to the original, geographically defined Sublicensed Territory. Specifically, section 2.1, which mandates WPD to 'commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory' now suffers from the uncertainty of what territory this refers to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory. In exchange for the payment by WPD of the Development Fee to CNS, CNS hereby agrees to pay WPD the following payments: (i) within thirty Business Days from the date of this Agreement, CNS will make an upfront payment of $225,000 to WPD; and (ii) within thirty days of the verified achievement of the Phase II Milestone, (such verification shall be conducted by an independent third party mutually acceptable to the parties hereto), CNS will make a payment of $775,000 to WPD.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Development Territory' with reference to the Sublicense Agreement and excluded Poland post-approval. The changed text defines 'Development Territory' as areas mutually agreed upon in writing, which can be adjusted. This introduces a contradiction because section 2.1 obligates WPD to commercialize products in the 'Development Territory' with a goal of eventual approval. The changed definition makes it unclear what territory that obligation applies to since it is subject to mutual agreement and adjustment, creating uncertainty and potentially conflicting obligations, especially since the Sublicense agreement's territory is no longer a determining factor.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 If after three years from the Effective Date of this Development Agreement, WPD fails to use commercially reasonable efforts as set forth in section 2.1 above, CNS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to the terms specified in Section 6.2 below, and CNS shall be entitled to the return of any payments made hereunder. For the purpose of this clause, if WPD has expended the funds provided by CNS pursuant to section 2.1 above on developing anti-viral indications (including all direct and indirect costs of such development), it will be deemed to have used commercially reasonable efforts in good faith.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 If after three years from the Effective Date of this Development Agreement, WPD fails to meet certain performance goals, CNS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to the terms specified in Section 6.2 below.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 2.2 provided a specific criterion for determining 'commercially reasonable efforts': expending the funds provided by CNS on developing anti-viral indications. The modified version replaces this with 'fails to meet certain performance goals,' without defining what those goals are. This creates ambiguity and makes it difficult to enforce the termination clause. It contradicts the spirit of providing measurable criteria for assessing WPD's performance, which was present in the original version. The undefined 'performance goals' now directly impact the clarity of the termination clause outlined in Section 6.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to any rights herein which survive termination, this Agreement will earlier terminate in its entirety: (i) upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice from either party if the other party materially breaches this Agreement, unless before the end of such thirty (30) calendar day notice period, the breaching party has cured the material default or breach to the non-breaching party's reasonable satisfaction; or (ii) at any time by mutual written agreement between the Parties, subject to any terms herein which survive termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear criterion for 'commercially reasonable efforts,' while the changed text introduces 'certain performance goals' without definition. This creates ambiguity, as Section 6.2 outlines termination rights based on breaches. The undefined 'performance goals' in the changed Section 2.2 directly conflict with the clarity expected for triggering the termination clause as described in the contradicted text, which specifies material breaches that can be cured. This makes enforcement uncertain, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
